behavior_summary: Presents a context-sensitive fire-magic UI to the player, composed of category submenus (offensive, defensive, other, pentagram) and individual spell entries, accepts the player's selection, and returns or invokes the corresponding spell/action code or incantation.
Scans the player's inventory and pentagram slots to count reagents and decide special-case behaviors (notably the 'Ignite' case) used to build menu content and to validate or alter casting behavior.

Provides helpers to map human-readable spell names to internal numeric IDs or to invoke the internal cast/handler; also includes an item stack-splitting helper used when reagents/items must be moved or spawned in the world.
notable_control_flow: FREE::0080 (Func_80) builds an slist (selection list) and repeatedly calls Item::ask to obtain the player's selection; the loop repeats until the player picks a non-empty entry or chooses 'Nevermind'.

Selection strings returned from Item::ask are compared against category labels and spell-name strings; matches are either translated to incantation strings (then returned) or converted to numeric result codes via mapping helpers.

Inventory/pentagram scanning (U8P_FREE::FREE::091E) iterates item lists, reads item frames/types, uses PENT::0F4B checks, and buckets reagent counts into byte counters that influence menu entries and special-case spells.

When a spell name is selected and requires direct invocation, U8P_FREE::FREE::0512 pushes two small numeric parameters and calls U8P_FREE::FREE::1A96 to perform the actual cast/handler invocation; the handler's return value is propagated back.
Loop/frame progression inside U8P_FREE::FREE::0895; treated as control-flow, not explicit internal call.
open_questions: Exact numeric semantics and full mapping table returned by U8P_FREE::FREE::071E (Func_71E) — the mapping of all spell-name strings to byte IDs is inferred but not fully enumerated.
Precise effects and parameter semantics of U8P_FREE::FREE::1A96 (the internal cast/handler) — inferred to perform casting but its full behavior and return codes are unknown.

Complete rules for 'Ignite' and other pentagram-specific behavior inside U8P_FREE::FREE::091E — the helper tallies reagents and has branches, but exact thresholds/conditions are not exhaustively documented.

The calling convention / initial inputs to FREE::0080 (which process/pid invokes it and with which contextual parameters) — the function appears to be invoked in a player/avatar context but the explicit param is not in the excerpt.

main_function.name: FREE::0080 (Func_80)
main_function.purpose: Offer a fire-magic selection menu to the player, build context-specific submenus (offensive, defensive, other, pentagram), obtain the player's choice, and convert that choice into a numeric result or trigger a spell-invocation string for downstream casting.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player activates a fire-magic item or UI (appears to run in the player/process context).
Runs when the engine needs to present an actionable spell list (based on inventory/pentagram contents and context).

function_map.name: U8P_FREE::0080
function_map.role: Orchestrates the fire-magic menu UI, handles player selection loops, and returns or routes the chosen spell/action.

function_map.name: U8P_FREE::0512
function_map.role: Translate a selected spell-name string into an invocation by calling the internal cast/handler (U8P_FREE::FREE::1A96) with small numeric parameters; returns handler retval.

function_map.name: U8P_FREE::071E
function_map.role: Map a spell-name string to a small numeric ID (lookup); used by the main flow to convert human-readable choices to internal codes.

function_map.name: U8P_FREE::0895
function_map.role: Handle item stack splitting and placement: decrement source stack or create/pop a single-quantity item at a nearby coordinate (used for reagent handling).

function_map.name: U8P_FREE::091E
function_map.role: Scan inventories and pentagram slots to count reagent types/frames, bucket counts into locals, and provide pentagram-aware decisions for menu construction and certain spells (e.g., 'Ignite').

function_map.name: U8P_FREE::1A96
function_map.role: Internal cast/handler invoked by U8P_FREE::FREE::0512 to perform the concrete spell effect or higher-level action; treated as an internal engine-facing routine.

internal_calls.purpose: Obtain reagent counts and pentagram-related info to decide which menu entries to present and whether special-cases (like Ignite) apply.
Convert selected spell-name strings into numeric IDs for returning to the caller or for further handling.
When a spell selection must be invoked rather than just mapped, forward the spell-name string to the invoker helper which calls the cast/handler.
Perform the actual spell invocation/handling by passing two small numeric parameters prepared from the matched spell mapping.

external_calls.purpose: Show the built selection list (slist) to the player and return the selected string pointer used to determine the spell/action.
Report an unexpected input or error message (e.g., 'Ooops.') to the player/process when mapping fails.
Read source item stack size to decide whether to decrement and spawn or to directly place a single item instance.
Adjust stack quantities after splitting (set the remaining quantity on the source or the new item).
Create a new item instance (single-quantity) when splitting a stack for reagent spawning/placement.
Place items into containers or the world at computed coordinates after creation/splitting.
Identify reagent types/frames while scanning inventories/pentagram slots to bucket counts.
Retrieve item type when needed during scanning or when creating items (used by stack helpers).
Test pentagram membership/slot properties of items while scanning; used to influence pentagram-specific menu entries and behavior.
Read coordinates of a source item to compute where to place the spawned/split item in the world.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Presents a context-sensitive fire-magic UI to the player, composed of category submenus (offensive, defensive, other, pentagram) and individual spell entries, accepts the player's selection, and returns or invokes the corresponding spell/action code or incantation.
